title: Herdr worker panes with KD-owned result capture
goal: Run authorized Pi workers inside named Herdr panes while preserving Khazad-Doom as the process-contract owner: KD builds the exact worker command, uses a KD-owned wrapper to capture stdout/stderr/exit/result artifacts, parses the same Pi contract output, and falls back to direct Pi execution when cockpit launch is unavailable.
acceptance: For Herdr-backed runs, each active slice worker is visible in a deterministically named Herdr pane tied to the run id and slice id, but Khazad-Doom still decides which slice is active and which worker command is authorized.

Herdr worker panes execute a KD-owned wrapper that launches Pi with the same resolved provider/model/profile/env/prompt/schema as the direct Pi runner and writes stdout, stderr, exit status, and result files under the run artifact directory.

Khazad-Doom parses worker results from KD-owned artifact files using the same Pi contract parser and JSON-only result validation as direct Pi execution; Herdr terminal rendering, pane scrollback, and agent-status metadata are never correctness inputs.
Cancellation remains daemon-owned: cancelling a run requests termination of the wrapped Pi process and records the same terminal/cancellation evidence as the direct runner path.

If Herdr pane startup or wrapper handoff fails before a worker is launched, the run falls back to the existing direct Pi runner and records a non-fatal cockpit fallback incident; if the wrapped Pi worker itself fails, the failure is treated like a normal worker failure, not hidden as a cockpit fallback.

Real-Herdr evidence proves a worker pane can run the wrapper and return a valid worker JSON result through artifact capture; normal cargo test remains portable unless the explicit Herdr e2e test is requested.
must_ask_if: The implementation would read worker JSON from Herdr pane text, terminal scrollback, or Herdr agent-status metadata instead of KD-owned artifact files.
The implementation would allow operator typing in worker panes to be treated as accepted slice evidence without an explicit manual-intervention marker.
The implementation would make Herdr responsible for retry policy, worker authorization, cancellation truth, verification, merge, or handoff.
The implementation would remove the direct Pi fallback path or make Herdr mandatory for headless runs.
The implementation would let Herdr pane/process liveness diverge from direct-run liveness, quiet-worker, or semantic-progress reporting instead of feeding the same daemon projection signals.
The implementation requires changing the Pi JSON output contract or relaxing worker result validation.
